trip.name: Chill Food and Photography in Hong Kong

days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring the local culinary scene

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Eiffel
timeline.detail: Arrive at Hotel Eiffel, a cozy place in Tsim Sha Tsui. It's just a 5-minute walk from the MTR station, perfect for exploring.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Eiffel, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Victoria Harbour
timeline.detail: Stroll along the Tsim Sha Tsui waterfront and enjoy stunning views of the Hong Kong skyline. It's one of the best places to take iconic photographs.

timeline.title: Dinner at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Savor a bowl of traditional wonton noodles at Mak's Noodle, a legendary spot known for its rich history and delicious food. Arrive early to avoid the line.

timeline.title: Auckland Street Night Market
timeline.detail: Immerse yourself in local culture at this vibrant night market. Sample street food like curry fishballs, and enjoy the lively atmosphere.
timeline.mapQuery: Auckland Street Night Market, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Nathan Road
timeline.detail: Walk along the famous Nathan Road and soak up the neon-lit atmosphere. Enjoy window shopping or grab a drink at a nearby café.

localTips: Buy an Octopus card at the airport for convenient travel on public transport.
Always check to see if restaurants accept cash or card prior to dining.
Visit the Hong Kong Tourist Information Centre to get a free city map and insights.

days.title: Cultural Exploration
days.subtitle: Discovering Hong Kong's heritage

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Experience a typical Hong Kong breakfast at this must-visit local eatery known for its creamy milk tea and classic breakfast sets.
timeline.mapQuery: Australia Dairy Company, Jordan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Explore one of the most famous temples in Hong Kong dedicated to the enshrined deity Wong Tai Sin, known for granting wishes.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Store
timeline.detail: Enjoy freshly made dim sum that captures the essence of local flavors. Don't miss their juicy siu mai and fluffy buns.

timeline.title: Hong Kong Museum of History
timeline.detail: Gain insights into Hong Kong's rich heritage through various exhibitions showcasing its past, culture, and traditions.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong Museum of History, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Shopping at PMQ
timeline.detail: Browse through unique shops and boutiques at PMQ, a creative hub once home to the police married quarters, now a vibrant marketplace.

timeline.title: Dinner at Yung Kee Restaurant
timeline.detail: Treat yourself to a meal at the famous Yung Kee, renowned for its roast goose. Experience fine Cantonese cuisine in an elegant setting.
timeline.mapQuery: Yung Kee Restaurant, Central, Hong Kong

localTips: Start your day early to avoid long queues at popular restaurants.
Ensure to dress modestly when visiting temples to respect local customs.
Carry a portable charger for your phone, especially during long days exploring.

days.title: Nature and Relaxation
days.subtitle: Enjoying scenic views

timeline.title: Breakfast at Café de Coral
timeline.detail: Enjoy a quick breakfast at this popular fast-food chain serving local favorites such as congee and toast.

timeline.title: Visit Wong Nai Chung Gap (Repulse Bay)
timeline.detail: Take a scenic walk in Repulse Bay with stunning views of the beach and surrounding hills, perfect for photography.

timeline.title: Lunch at The Boathouse
timeline.detail: Dine at The Boathouse which offers a relaxing atmosphere with views over the bay; try their seafood specialties.
timeline.mapQuery: The Boathouse, Repulse Bay, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Take the Peak Tram to Victoria Peak
timeline.detail: Ride the historic Peak Tram for spectacular views of the Hong Kong skyline and harbor. Don't forget your camera!

timeline.title: Enjoy the view at Sky Terrace 428
timeline.detail: Take in panoramic views over Hong Kong from the Sky Terrace 428 located at the Peak, a must-see for photographers.
timeline.mapQuery: Sky Terrace 428, Victoria Peak, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and Check Out
timeline.detail: Head back to your hotel to pack up, freshen up, and check out before departure.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Eiffel, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

localTips: Try to catch the sunset from Victoria Peak for stunning colors.
Book Peak Tram tickets online in advance to save time.
Be mindful of the weather, as it can change rapidly in the mountains.

hotels.name: Hotel Eiffel
hotels.note: 5-min walk from Tsim Sha Tsui MTR station, comfortable rooms.
hotels.address: Tsim Sha Tsui, Kowloon, Hong Kong

budget.note: Return flight from Penang to Hong Kong
Estimated spending on meals for 3 days

urgent.label: Book Peak Tram tickets
urgent.note: Online booking recommended to avoid queues.
